title: Youth running consensus statement: minimising risk of injury and illness in youth runners
population: Children and adolescent runners
notes: Core direct youth-runner boundary; no validated age-distance or clearance rule

title: Overuse Injuries, Overtraining, and Burnout in Young Athletes
population: Children and adolescent athletes
notes: Pain and overuse concern require clinician review; no product diagnosis or treatment

title: International Olympic Committee consensus statement on youth athletic development
population: Youth athletes across participation and performance levels
notes: Broad healthy inclusive sustainable participation and safeguarding boundary

title: IOC consensus statement on elite youth athletes competing at the Olympic Games: essentials to a healthy, safe and sustainable paradigm
population: Elite youth athletes approaching international competition
notes: Recent competition is context only and never recovery or readiness evidence

title: 2023 International Olympic Committee's (IOC) consensus statement on Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport (REDs)
population: Female and male athletes exposed to low energy availability
notes: Correction-aware extraction; CAT2 is clinician-facing and must not be automated

title: 2025 Update to the Female Athlete Triad Coalition Consensus Statement Part 1: State of the Science and Introduction of a New Adolescent Model
population: Adolescent and adult female athletes
notes: Sex-specific evidence retained; no sex-based product score or restriction

title: 2025 Update to the Female Athlete Triad Coalition Consensus Statement Part 2: Clinical Guidelines for Screening, Diagnosis, Treatment, and Return to Play for Adolescents and Adults
population: Adolescent and adult female athletes
notes: Clinical thresholds and return-to-play categories are not product automation inputs

title: The Male Athlete Triad-A Consensus Statement From the Female and Male Athlete Triad Coalition Part II: Diagnosis, Treatment, and Return-To-Play
population: Adolescent and young adult male athletes; especially endurance and weight-class sports
notes: Male sex is not protective; multidisciplinary medical assessment required

title: Recommended Amount of Sleep for Pediatric Populations: A Consensus Statement of the American Academy of Sleep Medicine
population: Healthy children and adolescents
notes: General sleep-duration guidance is not athlete readiness or injury clearance

title: International Olympic Committee (IOC) consensus statement on acute respiratory illness in athletes part 1: acute respiratory infections
population: Athletes with acute respiratory infection
notes: Youth directness low; illness and return-to-sport assessment remain clinician functions

title: The Adolescent Athlete and the Team Physician: A Consensus Statement. 2025 Update
population: Adolescent athletes ages 12-18
notes: Under-12 athletes outside scope; product cannot substitute for team physician

title: IOC consensus statement: interpersonal violence and safeguarding in sport
population: Athletes across ages and levels; children and youth explicitly considered
notes: Concern content routes outside planner to named trained safeguarding human

title: International Safeguards for Children in Sport
population: Children participating in sport from grassroots through elite
notes: Organizational standard with jurisdiction-specific implementation and reporting duties

title: Associations between growth, maturation and injury in youth athletes engaged in elite pathways: a scoping review
population: Elite youth athletes across pathways
notes: Heterogeneous methods; female athletes and sport diversity underrepresented

title: Skeletal maturation and growth rates are related to bone and growth plate injuries in adolescent athletics
population: Male elite adolescent athletics academy athletes; 117 athlete-seasons
notes: Small single-academy association study; IRRs are not individual risk scores

title: Identifying sex-specific risk factors for stress fractures in adolescent runners
population: Competitive high-school runners; 442 girls and 306 boys at baseline
notes: Low event counts; sex BMI and menarche findings must not become automated thresholds

title: Risk Factors for Running-Related Injury in High School and Collegiate Cross-country Runners: A Systematic Review
population: High-school and collegiate cross-country runners
notes: Training sleep and specialization evidence low certainty; no automated risk prediction

title: Risk Factors, Diagnosis and Management of Bone Stress Injuries in Adolescent Athletes: A Narrative Review
population: Adolescent athletes
notes: Heterogeneous incidence and recurrence ranges are not individual probabilities

title: Primary Periphyseal Stress Injuries in Young Athletes: A Systematic Review
population: Children and adolescents in repetitive youth sports
notes: Case-report dominant; symptoms require human assessment and imaging is not inferred

title: Lack of Sleep and Sports Injuries in Adolescents: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
population: Athletes age 19 or younger; seven studies
notes: Chronic association observed; acute evidence conflicting and not a readiness rule

title: Apophysitis and Osteochondrosis: Common Causes of Pain in Growing Bones
population: Growing children and adolescents with apophyseal or osteochondral pain
notes: Diagnosis and treatment remain clinical; product collects only explicit pain or limitation

title: Misunderstanding the female athlete triad: refuting the IOC consensus statement on Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport (RED-S)
population: Conceptual discussion focused on female athletes
notes: Opposing conceptual evidence; supports referral rather than automated model allegiance
